meaning: adj.无期限的,年代不详的,虽旧仍好的
examples: A dateless bargain to engrossing death!
A dateless bargain to engrossing death.
An error by the Royal Mint in Britain has seen the issue of tens of thousands of 'dateless' 20 pence COINS — driving their value up to 50 pounds (60 euros, 80 dollars) each, a collector said Monday.
Yet, though they were in principle temporal, a few became dateless, timeless.
Those who have none might feel "desperate and dateless, " he says.
